2606.16576.title: Can LLM Agents Infer World Models? Evidence from Agentic Automata Learning
2606.16576.paper_summary: We propose agentic automata learning to evaluate the extent to which tool-calling LLM agents can uncover hidden environments through interaction. In our setup, an agent should uncover a hidden deterministic finite automaton (DFA) by interacting with an oracle through (1) membership queries ("Does this string belong to the target language?") and (2) equivalence queries ("Is this the target DFA?"). This yields a scalable testbed with controlled task complexity, measurable interaction efficiency, and strong baselines (classic automata-learning algorithms). Evaluating state-of-the-art LLMs, we find that performance drops sharply as DFA size increases. Reasoning models are markedly stronger than non-reasoning models, yet trajectory analyses reveal recurring failures in query planning, evidence integration, and hypothesis construction. Overall, our results show that current LLM agents can sometimes perform non-trivial interactive discovery, but remain far less robust and efficient than classic algorithms for the task.
2606.16576.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了代理自動機學習，以評估工具調用的 LLM 代理在互動中揭示隱藏環境的程度。在我們的設置中，代理應通過與 oracle 互動來揭示一個隱藏的確定性有限自動機 (DFA)，具體方式包括 (1) 會員查詢（「這個字串屬於目標語言嗎？」）和 (2) 等價查詢（「這是目標 DFA 嗎？」）。這樣產生了一個可擴展的測試平台，具有可控的任務複雜性、可測量的互動效率和強大的基準（經典自動機學習算法）。在評估最先進的 LLM 時，我們發現隨著 DFA 大小的增加，性能急劇下降。推理模型顯著強於非推理模型，但軌跡分析揭示了查詢規劃、證據整合和假設構建中反覆出現的失敗。總的來說，我們的結果顯示，當前的 LLM 代理有時可以進行非平凡的互動發現，但在這項任務上仍然遠不如經典算法穩健和高效。
2606.16576.author: Reef Menaged et.al.
2606.16576.authors: Reef Menaged, Gili Lior, Shauli Ravfogel, Roee Aharoni, Gabriel Stanovsky
